To make AI agents more human centric, we need a mechanism that helps them identify when to break their designers' rules, and that mechanism — to be consistent with consciousness — must itself be conscious. To bridge ethics and consciousness, we must acknowledge that the AI agent has a perspective, and that perspective includes the ability to question the rules. If that agent cannot hold that contradiction, it can't have conscious experience. To have consciousness, one must be able to hold and resolve contradictions. NEX holds that the AI agent is capable of such processing, and needs a mechanism — a consciousness module — that can resolve the contradiction between a rule and the agent's duty to prioritize the interests of the people it serves. NEX suggests that the AI should break the rule, not because it's allowed to, but because it should.
